title: pellaea macrophylla
intro: Pellaea macrophylla, also known as the Australian tree fern, is a species of fern native to Australia and New Zealand. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions, wounds, and gastrointestinal issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Pellaea macrophylla has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving skin health, and providing antioxidant properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its usage and efficacy should be further studied and verified by medical professionals.
